seatPosition.name: Seat Position Visualizer
seatPosition.description: See how sliding or tilting your kart seat shifts front/rear weight and CoG height — with a calibration mode for corner scales.

seat.diagramAria: Side view of the kart showing seat position and centre of mass
seat.diagramLegend: Gray ghost = zero point · crosshair = combined CoG · dot = tilt anchor
seat.cogPosition: {{x}} mm fwd of rear axle · {{z}} mm high
seat.sensNaive: Naive "whole driver moves" estimate: {{value}}%/in — feet-on-pedals model is the lower number.
seat.cornerNote: Rigid-frame approximation — karts corner on frame jacking, so treat as a relative indicator.
seat.kartDriverNote: The baseline is your kart at the zero point — most sprint setups target 43% front (some run 40). Corner scales beat guesses: see Calibration.
seat.legCouplingNote: How much of the seat's movement the leg CoM follows (feet stay on the pedals). 0.4 is a sane default — calibrate it on scales.
seat.calibrationIntro: Default numbers are ±20% per driver. Two scale sessions anchor the model to your kart: weigh at the zero point, then slide the seat a known amount and re-weigh to fit the leg coupling.
seat.calStep1: 1 · Corner weights at the zero point (driver seated)
seat.calStep2: 2 · Slide the seat a known amount and re-weigh the front pair

stancePosition.name: Stance Visualizer
stancePosition.description: See how your foot placement, weight split and crouch set the front/rear balance — and the braking g at which you go over the nose.

stance.diagramAria: Side view of the board showing foot positions, the rider and the centre of mass
stance.diagramLegend: Crosshair = combined CoG · dashed plumb line = where the weight lands · foot marks scale with the split
stance.cogPosition: {{x}} mm fwd of the rear truck · {{z}} mm high
stance.riderShare: The rider is {{pct}}% of the moving mass.
stance.rearLifted: Rear wheels off the ground — past the endo point.
stance.wheelieValue: {{value}} g of acceleration lifts the front
stance.wheelieNote: Stand further back and you buy braking but lose traction under power — the two thresholds always trade against each other.
stance.lateralNote: A rider leans, so CoG height doesn't enter this one — the lateral limit is grip and bushings.
stance.boardRiderNote: Weigh yourself in your riding gear, including the backpack — it's ~85% of the mass and it's the part everyone guesses at.
stance.modelCoM: Rider CoM: 0.55 × your height above the deck (standard anthropometric figure), placed along the board at the load-weighted mean of your two feet. Board CoM: mid-wheelbase, about half deck height.
stance.modelEndo: Endo: moments about the front contact patch — the rear unloads completely at a = g · (wheelbase − x_cm) / z_cm.
stance.modelWheelie: Wheelie: moments about the rear contact patch — the front unloads at a = g · x_cm / z_cm. The two always sum to g · wheelbase / z_cm.
stance.modelCaveat: Rigid-body statics: no deck flex, no suspension, no bushing lean. Treat the thresholds as the ceiling a perfectly stiff board would have — a real one gets there sooner.
stance.budget: Your two thresholds always add up to {{value}} g. Where you stand splits that between nose and tail; crouching is what raises it.
stance.brakingLimitTitle: Hardest stop this board can make
stance.limitSlip: The rear wheels break traction and the board slides. Motor braking can't lift the rear: braking unloads the rear wheels, which are the ones doing the braking.
stance.limitEndo: The rear lifts before the tyres let go (~{{grip}} g of grip) — you pitch before you slide.
stance.endoAtUnreachable: Rear would lift at {{value}} g — a kerb or a pothole can do that to you. The motors can't.
stance.drivetrainNote: An eskate has no friction brakes — the motors are the brakes, so only the wheels they drive can slow the board.

landing.tileDescription: Trackside calculators and utilities — no datalog needed.
landing.noTools: No tools are available in this build.

laptimer.name: Lap Timer
laptimer.description: Turn your phone's GPS into a live lap timer — delta, lap times and sector splits, saved as a log you can review.
laptimer.endDialogBody: This stops logging and saves the session to your files. You can't add more to it afterward.
laptimer.savedHint: Find it in your saved files to review like any log.
laptimer.noDataBody: You need to be moving (above 5 mph) for a session to record.
laptimer.noLaps: No laps yet — complete a lap to see times here.
laptimer.speedometerMode: Speedometer mode — no tracks found nearby
laptimer.farHint: Data being logged for post-race analysis. Create a track here later to get lap times.
laptimer.waitingHint: Drive above 5 mph to start logging.
